q: We had just got off the bus when it began to rain.
a: No sooner had we got off the bus than it began to rain.

q: I had just got into the class when the teacher called my name.
a: No sooner had we got into the class than the teacher called my name.

q: We had just finished dinner when the lights went out.
a: No sooner had we finished dinner than the lights went out.

q: I had just started to study when the lights went out.
a: No sooner had I started to study than the lights went out.

q: The king had just crossed the bridge when it collapsed.
a: No sooner had the king crossed the bridge than it collapsed.

q: Mother had just put the clothes on the line when it started to rain.
a: No sooner had mother put the clothes on the line than it started to rain.

q: The dragon assumed his own form and made a dash for the sea.
a: No sooner did the dragon assume his own form than he made a dash for the sea.

q: They had just arrived home when the storm broke.
a: No sooner had they arrived home than the storm broke.

q: I had just put the clothes on the line when it started to rain.
a: No sooner had I put the clothes on the line than it started to rain.

q: We had just put on our rain coats when it stopped raining.
a: No sooner had we put on our raincoats than it stopped raining.

q: Toe Toe finished his breakfast and ran to catch the bus.
a: No sooner did Toe Toe finish his breakfast than he ran to catch the bus.

q: Su Su had just sat for the final examination when she took a computer course.
a: No sooner had Su Su sat for the final examination than she took a computer course.

q: When he sat at the table to study, he felt sleepy.
a: No sooner did he sit at the table to study than he felt sleepy.

q: Ma Latt had just finished sweeping the house when the guests arrived.
a: No sooner had Ma Latt finished sweeping the house than the guests arrived.

q: They had just got to the bus-stop when the bus left.
a: No sooner had they got to the bus-stop than the bus left.

q: When the teacher had left the room, everyone began to talk at once.
a: No sooner had the teacher left the room than everyone began to talk at once.

q: She had just finished her housework when her friend visited her.
a: No sooner had she finished her housework than her friend visited her.

q: We had just watered the plants when it began to rain.
a: No sooner had we watered the plants than it began to rain.
